text:
Here is the complete 6-email buyer lead drip sequence:

```json
[
  {
    "email_number": 1,
    "send_timing": "Immediately upon inquiry",
    "subject": "quick question about your home search, {first_name}",
    "body": "Hi {first_name},\n\nThanks for reaching out about homes in {area_of_interest}. I'm George Ramirez with the Jorge Ramirez Group at Keller Williams Premier Properties here in Central Jersey — I've helped over 500 families find the right home over the past 15+ years, so I know this market inside and out.\n\nBefore I send you anything, I'd love to understand what matters most to you. If you could only get ONE thing right about your next home — whether that's the neighborhood, the layout, the schools, the commute — what would it be?"
  },
  {
    "email_number": 2,
    "send_timing": "Day 3",
    "subject": "just curious on timing",
    "body": "Hi {first_name},\n\nNo rush on this at all — I just want to make sure I'm being helpful and not getting in the way. Everyone's on a different timeline and that's totally fine.\n\nIn a perfect world, when would you ideally like to be settled into your new place?"
  },
  {
    "email_number": 3,
    "send_timing": "Day 7",
    "subject": "something I meant to ask you",
    "body": "Hi {first_name},\n\nI find that the buyers I work with in {area_of_interest} usually have a story behind the move — a growing family, a job change, wanting to be closer to something that matters to them. Understanding that helps me know what to actually look for beyond just bedrooms and bathrooms.\n\nWould you mind sharing what's prompting the move for you right now?"
  },
  {
    "email_number": 4,
    "send_timing": "Day 12",
    "subject": "something interesting happening in {area_of_interest}",
    "body": "Hi {first_name},\n\nI was just reviewing recent activity in {area_of_interest} around the {price_range} range and noticed some trends worth knowing about — inventory levels have been shifting and homes in certain pockets are behaving very differently than others. What looks available online doesn't always tell the full story.\n\nAs you've been looking around, are you finding what you expected to see out there, or has the market surprised you a bit?"
  },
  {
    "email_number": 5,
    "send_timing": "Day 18",
    "subject": "thought you should know this",
    "body": "Hi {first_name},\n\nI don't say this to create any pressure — that's not how I operate. But I've been watching the {area_of_interest} market closely, and between rate fluctuations and inventory shifts, the homes available in the {price_range} range today may look quite different in 60-90 days. Buyers who waited last cycle often ended up paying more for less.\n\nHave you thought about what it would mean for your situation if your buying power changes or the homes you like now aren't available later?"
  },
  {
    "email_number": 6,
    "send_timing": "Day 24",
    "subject": "want me to set this up for you?",
    "body": "Hi {first_name},\n\nI know searching on your own can feel like drinking from a firehose — tons of listings, half of them outdated, and no way to tell what's actually worth your time. One thing I do for buyers I work with is set up a custom search that filters out the noise and only sends you homes that actually match what you're looking for in {area_of_interest} within {price_range}. No obligations, no strings — just better information.\n\nWould it be helpful if I put that together for you?"
  }
]
```

**Sequence summary:**

- **Email 1 (Day 0):** Warm welcome, establishes George's credibility (500+ homes, 15+ years), asks the priority question to get the buyer talking about what they actually care about.
- **Email 2 (Day 3):** Timeline discovery. Non-pressuring language ("no rush"), lets the buyer reveal urgency on their own terms.
- **Email 3 (Day 7):** Motivation discovery. Frames it as wanting to help beyond surface-level criteria — classic NEPQ "situation question" that gets the buyer to articulate their own reasons for moving.
- **Email 4 (Day 12):** Value-add market insight. Positions George as someone with knowledge they can't get from Zillow, then asks if reality matches expectations — a reverse-selling move that lets the buyer voice frustration or confusion.
- **Email 5 (Day 18):** Consequence/implication question. Not fear-based but honest. Lets the buyer connect the dots themselves about the cost of inaction — core NEPQ "consequence question."
- **Email 6 (Day 24):** Soft close. Offers something concrete and useful with zero commitment language. The ask is small ("would that be helpful?") which makes it easy to say yes.

Every email follows the rules: short (1-2 paragraphs), one question, casual subject lines, merge variables included, and no pushy language anywhere.
